methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
OSH Cut is a bootstrapped robotics and manufacturing company founded by Caleb Chamberlain. Valuation analysis is constrained by the absence of prior funding rounds and the limited availability of comparable entities at an equivalent stage. The relevant peer universe comprises 11 companies in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including 1 public entity (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms. Notable private peers include 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Standard peer-median methodology is not applicable here. A search for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables within the sector yielded zero datapoints meeting any filter criterion, falling below the minimum threshold of three required for reliable triangulation. Relying on all-sector medians would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors and business models represented in the broader peer set.

Without sufficient narrow-band comparables, traditional valuation approaches cannot be reliably applied. A credible valuation for OSH Cut would require either (1) expansion of the dataset through private market intelligence on earlier-stage robotics investments, (2) discounted cash flow analysis if sufficient operating and financial projections are available, or (3) analogical reasoning from the top-tier private peers listed, adjusted substantially for stage and maturity. The key uncertainties are revenue traction, capital efficiency, and market-size assumptions within the manufacturing robotics segment.
